steps.title: Identify given information and angle positioning
steps.content: We are given AB = DE = 5 cm and ∠A = ∠D = 35°. In triangle ABC, angle A is located at vertex A, positioned between sides AB and AC. In triangle DEF, angle D is located at vertex D, positioned between sides DE and DF. To prove congruence, we need one of the standard criteria: SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, or HL. Since we have one side and one angle, we should consider SAS (Side-Angle-Side), which requires two sides with the angle between them.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice against congruence criteria
steps.content:
• Choice A (BC = EF): This gives us AB = DE and ∠A with side BC, which is SSA. The angle A is not between sides AB and BC, so this configuration does not guarantee congruence.
  • Choice B (AC = DF): This gives us AB = DE (side), ∠A = ∠D (angle), and AC = DF (side). Since ∠A is between sides AB and AC, and ∠D is between sides DE and DF, this is the Side-Angle-Side configuration.
  • Choice C (∠B = ∠C): This only provides information about angles within triangle ABC and does not allow direct comparison with triangle DEF.
  • Choice D (no additional info): One side and one angle are not sufficient for any congruence criterion.
With AC = DF, we have SAS congruence: Side AB = DE, Angle A = D (included between the sides), Side AC = DF. That matches choice B — B is correct.
